title: Clara Finch - Interview 1
description: Clara gives efficient operational answers and seems eager to stabilize the investigation.

nodes.prompt: Walk me through your gala role.
nodes.response: Operations. Routes, vendors, doors, equipment, staff breaks. If anything moved, I probably knew about it.

nodes.prompt: Who knew Simon's alternate route?
nodes.response: Maryam handled his calendar, I handled the building routes. The alternate path was normal for donor nights because Simon hated crowded halls.

choices.label: Ask why Service Corridor C mattered.

nodes.prompt: Was the Memory Arch dangerous?
nodes.response: Noah was careful. I remember it being checked. But gala installs are chaos. Anything can become dangerous if people improvise.

nodes.prompt: Why was the arch placed in Service Corridor C?
nodes.response: Temporary staging. Lucas was available and the corridor was out of donor sight. It should have been harmless.

choices.label: Ask who checked it after Lucas moved it.

nodes.prompt: Why did you sign out Noah's toolkit?
nodes.response: Cable covers, signage clamps, minor event fixes. If I waited for every contractor, the gala would never open.

choices.label: Ask if you touched the Memory Arch.

nodes.prompt: Noah's photo shows the protective edge present at 5:55 PM.
nodes.response: Good. Then whatever happened after that belongs to the event floor, not Noah's shop.

choices.label: Ask who controlled the event floor.

nodes.prompt: Who checked the arch after Lucas moved it?
nodes.response: I cannot swear anyone did. We were understaffed. That is exactly why I hate last-minute program changes.

nodes.prompt: Did you touch the Memory Arch?
nodes.response: I handled the area, not the mechanism. There is a difference.

title: Clara Finch - Interview 2
description: Clara becomes precise in ways that sound rehearsed when the corridor evidence narrows.

nodes.prompt: You said your alibi near catering was continuous.
nodes.response: Because functionally it was. Ana saw me repeatedly. I was solving ten problems at once.

choices.label: Ask about Simon after the corridor strike.

nodes.prompt: Your badge entered the corridor storage alcove at 7:04 PM.
nodes.response: I retrieved supplies. That was my job. An access log is not a confession.

nodes.prompt: The Memory Arch's protective edge was missing after that window.
nodes.response: Then ask the fabricator, maintenance, or the volunteer who moved it. Operations does not mean omnipotence.

nodes.prompt: A fiber on the removed edge matches your staff lanyard.
nodes.response: My lanyard touches half this building. That is the problem with a job no one notices until it breaks.

nodes.prompt: What supplies did you retrieve?
nodes.response: Tape, spare clips, a sign bracket. I did not keep a poetry inventory.

nodes.prompt: Why did you discourage Sophie from treating Simon?
nodes.response: Because Simon was conscious. He answered questions. He never lost consciousness. The gala could not stop over every bump.

nodes.prompt: A lucid interval can look normal before a collapse.
nodes.response: I am not a doctor. Neither were most people treating the corridor like theater.

title: Clara Finch - Interview 3
description: Clara answers questions about accounts and Martin but denies any violent act.

nodes.prompt: Simon was reviewing duplicate vendor payments.
nodes.response: Simon reviewed many things he barely understood. He liked dramatic conclusions.

nodes.prompt: The referral draft names you.
nodes.response: Yes. I moved funds. I will not polish that. I was trying to keep Martin alive and housed. That is not the same as murder.

nodes.prompt: Martin's care facility threatened termination.
nodes.response: He cannot advocate for himself. I made terrible choices. I can say that without accepting your larger story.

nodes.prompt: The care warning makes the payments urgent.
nodes.response: It makes them desperate. Desperation is not a time machine, detective.

nodes.prompt: Did Simon threaten to fire or report you?
nodes.response: He said the gala would end and then my career would end. He did not have to spell out the rest.

title: Clara Finch - Final Interview
description: A gated confrontation sequence. The order matters: route, message, corridor presence, altered exhibit, motive, alibi.

nodes.prompt: Start with the route.
nodes.response: If you cannot explain how Simon reached Corridor C, you do not have a case.

nodes.prompt: The donor message redirected Simon.
nodes.response: A false message is ugly, but the institute lived on urgent donor messages.

nodes.prompt: The message uses your shorthand.
nodes.response: Half the staff copied my route codes because half the staff needed me to do their jobs.

nodes.prompt: Your badge entered the storage alcove before the strike.
nodes.response: Again, access is not intent.

choices.label: Present missing edge and Noah's inspection photo.

nodes.prompt: The edge was safe at 5:55 PM and missing after your alcove window.
nodes.response: You have a change. You still need a hand.

choices.label: Present lanyard fiber and reflective photo.

nodes.prompt: Fiber links your lanyard to the edge, and the photo places you near the corridor.
nodes.response: A building full of reflections and fibers. You know how small that sounds?

choices.label: Present the referral draft and Martin's care warning.

nodes.prompt: Simon planned to report you, and Martin's care payments were at risk.
nodes.response: I said I moved funds. I said why. I did not say I wanted Simon dead.

choices.label: Present Clara's statement and Ana's retraction.

nodes.prompt: Your alibi was not continuous, and your own words fit a lucid interval.
nodes.response: Then write your report carefully. A theory this complicated breaks if any part is guesswork.

title: Maryam Hart
description: Maryam explains how Simon was redirected.

nodes.prompt: Why did Simon go through Service Corridor C?
nodes.response: A donor message came in. It said Ezra needed a private word, and Corridor C was the fastest uncrowded path.

choices.label: Ask who knew the alternate route.

nodes.prompt: Who knew Simon used that route?
nodes.response: I did. Clara did. Security knew generally, but Clara knew the practical timing.

nodes.prompt: The shorthand resembles Clara's operations messages.
nodes.response: I noticed that later. At the time it looked normal because her shorthand was everywhere.

title: Noah Reed
description: Noah protects his inspection chain and points toward later handling.

nodes.prompt: Was the Memory Arch safe when you left it?
nodes.response: Yes. I photographed it because this place loves last-minute blame. Protective edge on, brake stop checked.

nodes.prompt: Who used your toolkit after inspection?
nodes.response: Operations signed it out. Clara's name is on the line. That does not mean she broke anything, but it was not with me.

nodes.prompt: The edge was missing after the strike.
nodes.response: Then someone removed it. It is not the kind of part that falls off because the building is moody.

title: Sophie Park
description: Sophie connects the corridor incident to medical risk.

nodes.prompt: What did you see after the corridor incident?
nodes.response: Simon was upright, but that can be deceptive. He hesitated, steadied himself, and refused help after Clara said he was fine.

nodes.prompt: Could he stay conscious and deteriorate later?
nodes.response: Yes. That is exactly why I offered help. Consciousness is not clearance.

nodes.prompt: What did Clara say?
nodes.response: That Simon never lost consciousness and the program needed him. It sounded practical at the time.

title: Ana Brooks
description: Ana clarifies that Clara's alibi was intermittent.

nodes.prompt: Was Clara with you continuously?
nodes.response: I said that because she asked me to keep it simple. But no. I saw her several times, not every minute.

nodes.prompt: How long were the gaps?
nodes.response: Long enough to fetch things. Long enough that I should not have used the word continuous.

nodes.prompt: What did you send Simon at 3:10 PM?
nodes.response: A ledger note. Duplicate vendor payments. I thought it was sloppy coding until I saw the destination chain.

nodes.prompt: How quickly did Simon understand it?
nodes.response: Fast. By 3:24 he had followed the trail. By the end of the afternoon he was using words like termination and referral.

nodes.prompt: Why would those payments matter?
nodes.response: The destination names were layered, but one account connected to Martin Finch's care facility.

title: Tessa Wynn
description: Tessa's photos compress the corridor window.

nodes.prompt: What did your corridor photos show?
nodes.response: At 6:52 PM the arch still had its edge. Later, a polished sign caught a reflection I almost deleted.

nodes.prompt: Who appears in the reflection?
nodes.response: It is not a portrait. But the height, blouse, and lanyard match Clara near the corridor at 7:41 PM.
